texto: The truth is, Socrates, that these regrets, and also the complaints about relations, are to be attributed to the same cause, which is not old age, but men's characters and tempers; for he who is of a calm and happy nature will hardly feel the pressure of age, but to him who is of an opposite disposition youth and age are equally a burden.
explicacao: Citação de Cephalus sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “The truth is, Socrates, that these regrets, and also the complaints about relations, are to be at…” articula reflexao, age, aging em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como aging e elderly.
